channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: How two Central football players are working to get recruited
item.description: A pair of local high school football stars -- Central High School's Jaime Padilla and Antonio Reyes -- are doing everything they can, even traveling the country to show college football programs what they can do.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Brewers catapulted by home runs as Mets bullpen squanders late lead
item.description: The Milwaukee Brewers were six outs away from going home, but two home runs in the bottom of the eighth inning forced a decisive Game 3 in the wild card series.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Higashioka's homer starts rally as Padres beat Braves 5-4 to sweep NL Wild Card Series
item.description: Kyle Higashioka’s solo homer started a five-run rally against Max Fried with two outs in the second inning, and the San Diego Padres held on to sweep the Atlanta Braves with a 5-4 win in Game 2 of their NL Wild Card Series.
item.dc:creator: Bernie Wilson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mitchell hits tiebreaking HR in the 8th, Chourio goes deep twice and Brewers rally past Mets 5-3
item.description: Jackson Chourio tied the game in the eighth with his second homer of the night and Garrett Mitchell delivered a two-run shot later in the inning to give the Milwaukee Brewers a 5-3 victory over the New York Mets that evened their NL Wild Card Series.
item.dc:creator: Steve Megargee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Orioles get swept out of the playoffs in their only series for a second consecutive year
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Things went so poorly for the Baltimore Orioles in the playoffs that even when they had the bases loaded and a hit by pitch scores a run, Colton Cowser swung at a ball thrown right at him, missed and broke his left hand in the process.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lionel Messi, Inter Miami win MLS Supporters Shield with 3-2 win at Crew. It's Messi's 46th trophy.
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Lionel Messi and Inter Miami have another trophy, along with home-field advantage throughout the Major League Soccer playoffs.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Wife of Rams GM fires back at fan pressuring her to 'tell him what to do' with Davante Adams trade
item.description: One desperate Los Angeles Rams fan may have crossed the line with the wife of general manager Les Snead over a potential trade involving Raiders star Davante Adams.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Witt gets another big hit to send the Royals into ALDS with a 2-game sweep of the Orioles
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Bobby Witt Jr. beat out an infield single to drive in the go-ahead run and send the Kansas City Royals into an AL Division Series with a 2-1 victory over the Baltimore Orioles on Wednesday for a two-game sweep of their Wild Card Series.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bills place emphasis on defensive backups in wake of NFL's 4-game suspension of Von Miller
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — Bills cornerback Rasul Douglas didn’t think anything of Von Miller's absence at practice on Wednesday, assuming the edge rusher had a rest day, as sometimes happens for the 35-year-old veteran.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bucs remain banged up but carry confidence into Thursday's Falcons clash
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers are riding momentum into Thursday night’s NFC South clash with the Atlanta Falcons but so far, the short game week has presented many challenges.
item.dc:creator: Dan Lucas, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Tragedy of Peter Edward Rose
item.description: Pete Rose, in many ways, bookended my life and career – he was my first hero as a kid, and I saw him break Ty Cobb's hit record. Now, he's gone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Megan Rapinoe condemns journalist for asking player about giving Caitlin Clark black eye: 'That feels racist'
item.description: Former women's soccer player Megan Rapinoe spoke out against USA Today journalist Christine Brennan for asking DiJonai Carrington a question about poking Caitlin Clark's eye.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dream fire Tanisha Wright in WNBA's 3rd coach firing since playoffs began
item.description: The Atlanta Dream have fired coach Tanisha Wright after three seasons, the franchise announced Wednesday. Wright took over the team in 2022 and helped them reach the playoffs the last two seasons. The Dream lost both years in the opening round of the postseason, including to New York last month.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jets' Lazard expects NFL to fine him over gun-like celebration
item.description: FLORHAM PARK, N.J. (AP) — New York Jets wide receiver Allen Lazard expects to be fined by the NFL for his celebration after a catch against Denver last Sunday that included what officials deemed to be a gun-like gesture. “It’s something that’s very silly,” he said Wednesday. “Very, very silly.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Knicks add Karl-Anthony Towns from Timberwolves after completing three-team trade with Hornets
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Karl-Anthony Towns joined the Knicks on Wednesday after the Minnesota Timberwolves sent their four-time All-Star to New York in a three-team trade that also included the Charlotte Hornets.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alcaraz rallies past Sinner to win China Open and make it 3-for-3 against his top rival this year
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Carlos Alcaraz won seven straight points in the decisive tiebreaker to beat top-ranked Jannik Sinner 6-7 (6), 6-4, 7-6 (3) and win the China Open on Wednesday, extending his recent mastery over his biggest rival — who is dealing with an ongoing doping case.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jason Kelce blasts statistics in wild rant: 'Stats are numbers, numbers are nerds, nerds are losers'
item.description: Jason Kelce launched a tirade against stats saying, "Stats are numbers, numbers are nerds, nerds are losers, therefore stats are losers," during a recent episode of "New Heights.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NCAA March Madness returning to San Francisco
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- March Madness is returning to San Francisco, to Chase Center, specifically. As previously announced, the venue will host the 2025 NCAA Division I Men's Basketball West Regionals on March 27 and 29 of 2025.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Indian soccer team refuses to travel to Iran for game because of security fears
item.description: KOLKATA, India (AP) — India’s Mohun Bagan Super Giants has refused to travel to Iran to play Tractor SC in an Asian Champions League Two match scheduled for Wednesday because of the escalating security problems in the country and region.
item.dc:creator: KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Team president Jed Hoyer disappointed about 2024, but optimistic about Cubs' direction
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The 2024 MLB playoffs began Tuesday, and the Cubs were once again not among the six teams in the National League playoff field.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tom Brady to announce 49ers-Cardinals game Sunday
item.description: (KRON) -- Tom Brady will announce his first San Francisco 49ers game on Sunday, according to his page on Fox's website. The ex-player considered the Greatest Of All Time by many made the switch to the announcing booth this season.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: High School Boys Golf: Grand Junction shines in regionals, state tournaments set
item.description: This week, the 4A Region 4 tournament took place at Black Canyon Golf Course in Montrose. If we learned anything, it's that the Grand Junction Tigers are for real.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Jazz new additions bring experience and leadership
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Jazz did not land the "big game" Danny Ainge talked about in April, but they did sign a few key veteran role players this summer. Patty Mills is about to begin his 16th NBA season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Are professional athletes playing too many games?
item.description: Fans, athletes and medical experts have been having discussions on the increasing demand for players starting from the time they first start playing, all the way up to the professional ranks.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, Fox 5

item.title: Favre tries to expand his defamation lawsuit against Mississippi auditor over welfare spending
item.description: Favre is not facing criminal charges, but he is among more than three dozen people or businesses the state is suing to try to recover misspent money through the Temporary Assistance for Needy Families program.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: College GameDay makes first-ever appearance at Berkeley
item.description: (KRON) -- Since taking its show on the road back in 1993, "College GameDay" has been a huge presence in the college football community by being known to travel across dozens of campuses across the country.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: WWE NXT heads to Chicago for live debut on CW Network
item.description: The event marks a significant moment for WWE NXT as it expands its reach to a broader audience on the CW Network.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Morales, NewsNation

item.title: 2025 Club World Cup final to be at MetLife Stadium, site of 2026 World Cup final
item.description: Next year’s championship of the expanded Club World Cup will be played on July 13 at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey, site of the 2026 World Cup final.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hoop Dreams turns 30 as stars reflect on groundbreaking documentary
item.description: CHICAGO — Thirty Years ago, the sports documentary Hoop Dreams was released in theaters, quickly becoming a sensation. Acclaimed film critic Roger Ebert called it "one of the best films about American life I have ever seen.
item.dc:creator: Josh Frydman, WGN 9

item.title: Quarterback of undefeated college team says he will no longer play over unmet 'representations'
item.description: He claims it had become clear that "certain representations" made to him "would not be fulfilled in the future.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, NewsNation

item.title: Derrick Rose, Chicago native and NBA MVP with the Bulls, announces retirement from pro basketball after 16 seasons
item.description: CHICAGO — Derrick Rose, a Chicago native and city basketball legend in every sense of the word, is retiring from professional basketball. Rose, who grew up in Englewood on the city's Southwest Side and was a prep star at Simeon before being drafted No.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Oakland A's play last game at Coliseum Thursday afternoon
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (KRON) -- After 57 years of Major League Baseball, the Oakland A's will play their last game at the Oakland Coliseum on Thursday afternoon. They will take on the Texas Rangers at 12:37 p.m. The A's are headed to Sacramento next season, and they plan to eventually go to Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4
